rationale.R1.1: Positioning against prior work centrally requires reframing the paper's gap and contribution relative to adjacent literatures.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation is load-bearing on estimating how the work differs from and improves on nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.7: Prior-work positioning requires attention to whether the relevant literatures are accurately covered and attributed.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because claims such as firstness and nearest-prior-work comparisons depend on the completeness of the literature view.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because positioning language can easily inflate novelty beyond what the cited or known prior work supports.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active because the task is to project responsibility for positioning, not to rewrite or evaluate the whole paper.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active to avoid endorsing unsupported novelty claims that could mislead readers or reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are active because prior-work comparisons should remain tied to identifiable works, evidence, and attribution paths.
